steps: Find the greatest common factor of 8x^10, -8x^9, and 88x. Each coefficient is divisible by 8, and each term has at least one factor of x, so GCF = 8x.
explanation:
• Choice A — '$x(7x^{10} - 7x^9 + 87x)$': Factoring out only x gives x(8x^9 - 8x^8 + 88), not 7x^10 - 7x^9 + 87x. The coefficients are wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — '$x(8^{10} - 8^9 + 88)$': This treats the exponents as bases (8^10 instead of x^10), which is not what factoring produces. ✗
  • Choice C — '$8x(x^{10} - x^9 + 11x)$': Factoring out 8x from 8x^10 gives x^9 (not x^10), and from 88x gives 11 (not 11x). The exponents are off by one. ✗
  • Choice D — '$8x(x^9 - x^8 + 11)$': 8x(x^9) = 8x^10, 8x(-x^8) = -8x^9, 8x(11) = 88x. Multiplying back confirms this is correct. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
